name: Chocolate Truffle Cake-Coyote Grill
recipeIngredient: 2 lbs Bittersweet chocolate (best quality); broken into small pieces
1/4 cup Bourbon whiskey (or liquor of your choice)
recipeInstructions:
Heat the heavy cream in a double boiler over simmering water until it is just warm to the touch. Add the chocolate and stir constantly until completely melted and smooth. This can take 12-15 minutes. Refrigerate this ganache in the pan until set, about 4 hours.
Line a 9-inch round pan with plastic wrap.
With a heavy spoon, remove three-quarters of the ganache to the bowl of an electric mixer or a large food processor fitted with the metal blade. Do not use a hand mixer or a standard-size food processor—the motors are not strong enough to process the ganache. Refrigerate the remaining ganache.
Add the bourbon to the ganache in the bowl and mix at low speed until combined.
Continue beating or processing; the mixture will get softer (You may need to stop the food processor several times to break up and reposition the ganache until it begins to soften). As the ganache softens, increase the mixer speed to medium. After it softens, the mixture will lighten in color, then start to thicken or tighten again. Stop processing at this point; otherwise, the mixture may become grainy.
Transfer the mixture to the lined cake pan, spreading evenly with a knife. Place a piece of plastic wrap over the ganache and press down to eliminate air pockets. Remove the plastic covering the top and refrigerate for 1 hour.
To complete the cake before serving, invert the cake pan onto a serving dish. Use the reserved ganache from the refrigerator and place it in a heavy saucepan over very low heat. Stir constantly until melted and warm (not hot), as it will melt the cake. Pour the melted ganache over the cake, spreading so that it flows down the sides. Refrigerate again for at least an hour.
Remove any ganache on the serving dish. Use a pastry tube to pipe whipped cream rosettes around the edge of the plate.
